input.background: Each year, cardiologists surgically repair thousands of clogged coronary arteries only to have many of these arteries narrow again (restenose) soon after surgery. A study sponsored by the NIH was conducted to help determine whether infection with a common type of virus, cytomegalovirus (CMY), contributed to coronary restenosis. Forty-nine of the subjects showed serological evidence of CMV infection, while 26 showed no such evidence. In the CMV+ group, 21 individuals restenosed within 6 months of atherectomy. In the CMV — group, 2 individuals restenosed.
input.question: Calculate the risk in the CMV+ group and the CMV- group, and find the risk difference.

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer: Risk in the CMV+group \( \hat{p}_1=\frac{21}{49}=0.4286 \) Risk in the CVM - group \( \hat{p}_2=\frac{2}{26}=0.0769 \) Risk difference \( \hat{p}_1-\hat{p}_2=0.4286-0.0769=0.3517 \)

CMV_positive_group_restenosis.description: Number of individuals in the CMV+ group who experienced restenosis within 6 months.

CMV_positive_group_total.description: Total number of individuals in the CMV+ group.

CMV_negative_group_restenosis.description: Number of individuals in the CMV- group who experienced restenosis within 6 months.

CMV_negative_group_total.description: Total number of individuals in the CMV- group.
